last_safe_checkpoint.description: Validated max-iteration loop control, default 50-attempt behavior, and eager exit on supervisor approval.

bootstrap.goal: Add user-facing max-iteration loop control, default it to 50 total attempts, and stop Dormammu as soon as development is approved.

task_sync.resume_checkpoint: Max-iteration loop-control validation is complete. Resume from commit preparation or the next scope.

items.text: Phase 1. Review loop-run and resume semantics to map a user-facing max-iteration budget onto the existing retry model safely
Phase 2. Add --max-iterations to loop commands and default the total attempt budget to 50 when users do not set a loop budget explicitly
Phase 3. Preserve immediate Dormammu exit on supervisor approval and prevent resume from running again after the total iteration budget is spent
Phase 4. Add focused tests and docs for default budget, explicit iteration limits, resume overrides, and early exit on completion
Phase 5. Run automated validation and a local smoke test under ~/samba/test/dormammu-max-iterations-smoke

next_action: Prepare a commit for the validated max-iteration loop-control change, or begin the next user-directed scope.
notes: Resume from the validated max-iteration loop-control slice unless a new higher-priority roadmap slice overrides it.
Legacy TASKS.md snapshots should be migrated forward into PLAN.md without losing checklist state.
Loop commands now support a total iteration budget through --max-iterations and default to 50 total attempts when not configured explicitly.
Supervisor approval still exits Dormammu immediately instead of consuming the remaining loop budget.
Validation passed through targeted unit tests covering CLI, loop runner, config, and install behavior.

latest_commit.summary: Refine workflow and commit guidance for test authoring
